content: The human brain’s active memory mechanism, which continuously revises and updates old memories to integrate new learning, can be repurposed as a built-in curiosity regulator for autonomous AI agents: infrequently accessed, outdated stored experiences are flagged as low-information "unexplored" environmental gaps to drive intrinsic exploration, rather than discarding them as irrelevant noise. This replaces separate static curiosity modules like ICM or RND with a memory-native intrinsic reward system.
source_topics: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
trigger_topic: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
